primary.axis_differ: I2S substitution (cmplog has it; naive does not). Predicted that under indirect-dispatch the technique would add throughput tax with no yield, inverting the usual cmp>naive direction.

hypothesis.mechanism: An array-of-function-pointers dispatch indexed by an input-derived tag emits NO icmp eq and NO switch at the LLVM IR level. The discriminator resolves through an indirect call through memory, not a value comparison. Therefore none of sancov's value-side hooks (__sanitizer_cov_trace_const_cmp1/2/4/8, __sanitizer_cov_trace_cmp1/2/4/8, __sanitizer_cov_trace_switch) is emitted on the dispatch. Predicted that cmplog would lose to naive because cmplog pays tracing-stage overhead via N_NOISE_PER_HANDLER literal-equality CMPs in the non-target arm without any compensating I2S yield on the dispatch.
hypothesis.program_feature_parameter: N_HANDLERS (table size). Trap rate per execution = 1/N. Throughput-tax per execution is roughly constant (driven by N_NOISE_PER_HANDLER in non-target arm). Predicted cmp/naive ratio FLAT across N; absolute counts ~1/N.
hypothesis.predicted_direction: naive > cmp at every scan value. cmp/naive ratio approximately constant in N.

predicted_4fuzzer_signatures.naive: highest at every N; ~1/N scaling, no extra tax
predicted_4fuzzer_signatures.cmplog: below naive at every N; same 1/N scaling, multiplied by ~0.2-0.5 from throughput tax
predicted_4fuzzer_signatures.value_profile: tracks naive (gradient on bytes 4-7 noise compares doesn't reach the dispatch)
predicted_4fuzzer_signatures.value_profile_cmplog: tracks cmp (combined tax, no extra signal)

summary.predicted_shape: RBRB (naive R, cmp B, vp R, vpc B)
summary.observed_shape: BBRR (naive B, cmp B, vp R, vpc R) — value_profile axis dominates I2S axis at every N
summary.naive_vs_cmp: TIE at every N. cmp/naive ratio ≈ 1.0 across N=4 (3/3), N=16 (9/8), and N=64/256 (both fuzzers at 0). The predicted throughput tax from cmplog instrumentation on N_NOISE_PER_HANDLER compares is NOT measurable in this synthetic at this duration. cmp neither helps (no I2S signal on dispatch) nor hurts (no measurable overhead).
summary.vp_vs_vpc: TIE at small N (n=4: 16 vs 22, n=16: 68 vs 87 — vpc slightly ahead), vp pulls ahead at n=64 (138 vs 58). vp ≈ vpc at n=256 (105 vs 100). The predicted vp>vpc direction emerges only at one N (n=64); not a clean monotone curve.
summary.vp_dominates_naive_cmp: vp/vpc dominate naive/cmp at every N by orders of magnitude (n=4: 5×–7×, n=16: 8×–10×, n=64: ∞ vs 0, n=256: ∞ vs 0). This is the dominant unpredicted finding.
summary.mechanism_correction: The handler_noop function contains 16 literal-equality compares against NOISE_MAGICS at bytes 4–7. value_profile retains seeds in distinct CMP_MAP buckets per compare per hamming distance, enriching the corpus far beyond naive's edge-only retention. The enriched corpus carries more diverse byte-0 values across mutation-eligible seeds, accelerating random walk on the dispatch tag — the only path to TARGET_TAG. cmplog's I2S splices NOISE_MAGICS values into bytes 4–7 (where they were observed), which gives no benefit on byte 0 (the dispatch tag is uncomparable). Net: cmp ≈ naive (no benefit, no measurable tax); vp ≈ vpc (corpus enrichment dominates).
summary.naive_cliff_at_n64: naive=cmp=0 at n=64 and n=256 across all 5 trials. At n=4 naive saturates at 3 unique crash hashes; at n=16 grows to 5–10. The cliff at n=64 reflects that LibAFL records UNIQUE minimized crash inputs (content-hash dedup) — without value-side feedback to grow corpus diversity, naive/cmp's queue stays small enough that the few seeds that mutate byte 0 to TARGET_TAG produce too few unique inputs to register as separate crashes within the budget.

verification.verdict_rationale: Acceptance rule (params.json): 'naive median > cmp median at EVERY N_HANDLERS scan value; vp median > vpc median at every N (RBRB shape preserved across the sweep)'. The naive>cmp clause FAILS at every N (always tied or cmp slightly ahead). The vp>vpc clause FAILS at n=4 and n=16 (vpc ahead) and n=256 (tied); only holds at n=64. Both clauses of the predicted RBRB direction are refuted. The mechanism (indirect dispatch is opaque to value-side hooks) is correct in mechanism but wrong in consequence: opacity neither helps nor hurts cmp materially; the bigger force is value_profile's corpus-enrichment via the noise compares.
notes: Replaces refuted i2s_jump_table_opacity. Original premise (jump-table dispatch invisible to cmplog) was disproven by inspecting LibAFL's __sanitizer_cov_trace_switch hook in libafl_targets/src/sancov_cmp.c:53-87, which iterates all case literals into the cmplog dictionary.
True opacity requires absence of icmp/switch IR — function-pointer dispatch is the canonical example. Other unhookable idioms: C++ vtable, computed goto, JIT-emitted code.

No instance of this mechanism exists in the canonical 5-target DB. Real-world examples are common in C++ codebases (virtual dispatch) and C codebases with method-table patterns (Linux kernel ops structs, OpenSSL EVP, etc.) — not in our currently-instrumented set.
Trap is a direct __builtin_trap() in handler_trap with NO upstream gating — eliminates confound with any other I2S/VP-relevant comparison.

REFUTATION (2026-05-09): The harness DOES achieve sancov opacity on the dispatch as designed (verified by inspecting the IR / disassembly: TABLE[tag](...) emits an indirect call, no trace_const_cmp4 on the dispatch byte). But the predicted CONSEQUENCE — that this opacity flips cmp<naive — does not hold. Instrumenting the dispatch is not what makes cmp win in the canonical i2s_magic_number_gate regime; the win comes from cmp's I2S splicing the literal at the input offset where it was compared. Removing the compare removes the WIN, but doesn't create a LOSS. cmplog instrumentation overhead per non-target execution turns out to be too small to measure against naive at this duration.

UNEXPECTED FINDING: value_profile-based fuzzers dominate naive/cmplog at every N. The 16 noise compares in handler_noop give value_profile rich CMP_MAP feedback, leading to corpus enrichment that indirectly accelerates random walk on the dispatch byte. This is essentially i2s_corpus_pollution running in REVERSE — corpus enrichment via downstream noise CMPs HELPS instead of hurting because there's no chain of useful CMPs to dilute. The actual decisive shape is BBRR (axis = value_profile), aligning the template with the corpus_pollution mechanism family rather than the predicted I2S-hurts/jump-table family.

Methodological lesson: 'opaque to cmp's I2S' ≠ 'cmp loses to naive'. To force cmp to LOSE, the harness needs an actively penalizing factor (corpus pollution, anchored monoculture) — pure absence of I2S signal yields TIE, not LOSS. Future templates targeting RBRB should use the i2s_anchored_seed_deviation_trap or i2s_corpus_pollution mechanism, not pure opacity.
